text:
When setting project goals, it is important to use the SMART framework. This means your goals should be Specific, Measurable, Achievable, Relevant, and Time-bound. For example, instead of saying "improve the website," you could set a SMART goal: "Increase website traffic by 20% within three months."

Break the project into milestones. These are smaller steps that help you track progress and stay on schedule. Each milestone should have clear deliverables, such as a completed report or a new feature. Finally, ensure stakeholder alignment by discussing goals with everyone involved. When all parties agree on the objectives, the project is more likely to succeed.
